title: Asuka stars in a manga tutorial book
display_title: Asuka stars in a manga tutorial book
wikitext:
[]: Asuka stars in a manga tutorial book             Quote Post by Miuzaki » Wed Jun 01, 2011 6:55 pm You would have to buy it and see for yourself but the proof is in the text. Art of Drawing Manga, on google books You can preview it. Yes you heard right. Although its made By Sergi Cámara, Vanessa Durán, a few foreign artists. Asuka stars on page 37. The images are copywright protected by google but the text tells you plainly. It is depicting characters from real series. Its an extremely helpful book in other terms. Id like a new way of learning to draw guidelines I know im buying.

[]: Asuka was too tsundere for Google so they blocked her out.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jun 03, 2011 11:21 am This actually looks like a well-informed and useful book, unlike every other "how to draw manga" book on the market written by Americans who can't actually draw manga. It's really nice that they've gone through the effort to use images from real series and not just generic stuff. I might have to pick this up, even though I can't draw for crap.

[]:             Quote Post by Miuzaki » Fri Jun 03, 2011 11:37 am If you just have a willing attitude you will improve very quickly. Always be on your toes and try new things. This is how i improved very quickly.

[]: Asuka stars in a manga tutorial book             Quote Post by Miuzaki » Wed Jun 01, 2011 6:55 pm You would have to buy it and see for yourself but the proof is in the text. Art of Drawing Manga, on google books You can preview it. Yes you heard right. Although its made By Sergi Cámara, Vanessa Durán, a few foreign artists. Asuka stars on page 37. The images are copywright protected by google but the text tells you plainly. It is depicting characters from real series. Its an extremely helpful book in other terms. Id like a new way of learning to draw guidelines I know im buying.

[]: Asuka was too tsundere for Google so they blocked her out.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jun 03, 2011 11:21 am This actually looks like a well-informed and useful book, unlike every other "how to draw manga" book on the market written by Americans who can't actually draw manga. It's really nice that they've gone through the effort to use images from real series and not just generic stuff. I might have to pick this up, even though I can't draw for crap.
